Skeleton-to-graph root ordering pipeline (pixel units)

Source: R/RootBranching.R
root_graph_pipeline.Rd

Core engine: crops to the foreground, computes the distance transform, traces segments with junction contraction, resolves crossings, optionally prunes weak tips, and assigns all three order schemes. Returns a per-segment edge table in pixels; use branch_order_map for a unit-aware wrapper.

Usage

root_graph_pipeline(
  skel = NULL,
  mask = NULL,
  verbose = TRUE,
  dt_backend = "auto",
  crop = TRUE,
  overlay_png = NULL,
  max_side = 2000,
  keep_segments = FALSE,
  resolve_overlaps = TRUE,
  crossing_straight = -0.5,
  color_by = c("branch_order", "root_order", "tip_order"),
  diam_weight = 0.5,
  prune_min_length = 0,
  prune_min_diameter = 0,
  prune_iter = 0L
)

Arguments

skel

Binary skeleton: single-layer SpatRaster (preferred) or 0/1 matrix. Do not pre-convert a raster with as.matrix(). If NULL, it is computed from mask via skeletonize_image().

mask

Filled root mask on the same grid for the distance transform. Required if skel is NULL.

verbose

Print progress.

dt_backend

Distance-transform backend: "auto", "imager", or "baseR".

crop

Crop to the foreground bounding box (with a 1-px pad) first.

overlay_png

Optional path for the validation PNG.

max_side

Long-side cap (px) for the overlay.

keep_segments

Attach the traced segments as attr(., "segments") (needed for re-plotting and classification maps).

resolve_overlaps

Resolve degree-4 crossings by continuity.

crossing_straight

Straightness threshold for crossing resolution.

color_by

Which order column the overlay colours by.

diam_weight

Diameter-vs-angle weight for the root-continuation choice.

prune_min_length, prune_min_diameter, prune_iter

Optional terminal-segment pruning (off when prune_iter = 0); see prune_terminal_segments.

Value

A per-segment edge table (data.frame) with order and diameter columns in pixels, carrying attrs crop_offset, dims, and (if requested) segments.

Details

Three order columns are produced (see branch_order_map for the full rules): tip_order (per-segment leaf-peeling), root_order (per-root max tip_order), branch_order (per-root centrifugal generation from the thickest root). color_by selects which one the overlay uses.

See also

branch_order_map

On this page

Developed by Johannes Cunow.
